feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: IW21 Notification app on the SAP Fiori Launchpad is not mobile responsive.
entry.content: Transaction IW21 (Create Maintenance Notification) has been added to the SAP Fiori Launchpad via a custom tile using a custom application ID. When accessed from mobile devices (smartphones/tablets), the application does not adapt to the screen size and is not responsive. Users are required to scroll horizontally to view the full content of the screen.

entry.title: Header-Level Cost Distribution for Partial Goods Receipt
entry.content:
We need to open a raise ticket with SAP before the end of the day regarding a spare parts purchasing scenario.Business Scenario:

  • A Purchase Order (PO) was created for 100 spare parts.
  • Only 80 items have been delivered and will be received through a partial Goods Receipt (GR).

Requirement:
We need to distribute the header-level costs (such as planned delivery costs or other header charges) only across the 80 received items, rather than allocating the costs to all 100 PO items.Could you please advise whether there is a standard SAP solution to achieve this at the header level, without manually distributing the costs at the item level

entry.title: Putaway bin proposal for multiple fixed bins
entry.content:
During the fixed bin putaway scenario, if a product has multiple fixed bins assigned, our standard understanding is that the system proposes the bin that comes first based on the sort sequence.

However, our requirement is that when multiple fixed bins are assigned to a particular product, the bin that was added first in the /scwm/binmat should be proposed for putaway, instead of the bin determined by the sort sequence.

Kindly confirm whether this behavior is supported in standard functionality. Also, please let us know if there is any BAdI or enhancement option available to achieve this requirement.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  • Check the sort sequence maintained for the fixed bins in storage type X.
  • In /SCWM/BINMAT, assign multiple fixed bins to the same product for the particular st type.
  • Ensure the fixed bins are created at different times so that each assignment has a different creation timestamp. In a test case, BIN6 was assigned first, followed by BIN2, BIN4, etc.
  • Create the inbound delivery for the product.
  • Create the putaway Warehouse Task (WT) for the inbound delivery.Observe the destination bin proposed by the system:
  • Actual Result: The system proposes BIN2, which has the smallest sort sequence among the assigned fixed bins.
  • Expected Result: The system should propose BIN6, as it was the first fixed bin assigned to the product in /SCWM/BINMAT, regardless of the sort sequence.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Personal Settings in ME21N etc. (Tab. ESDUS) empty after conversion
entry.content:
Hello,

Our R/3 system has been converted to an S/4HANA sandbox system.
Unfortunately, the personal settings for MM transactions, which are stored in table ESDUS, are no longer available after the conversion.
I could not find any documentation stating that this is standard behavior.

Is there a standard SAP procedure, report, or migration tool available to transfer or restore ESDUS entries during or after an S/4HANA system conversion?

Many thanks!

entry.title: Ability to Ship Quality Stock from Plant to Plant
entry.content: Is there any solution to ship Quality Stock from our 3PL to another plant? Are there any standard or customization process in SAP that allow us to move stock type quality from plant to plant.

entry.title: Reg: Incorrect BOM usage in quantity structure of cost estimate.
entry.content:
After changing the BOM status to inactive of a costing BOM, we noticed that the Quantity structure in cost estimate screen picked a strange BOM usage as 9. 
We don’t have any BOM created for any of the material with usage 9 so far. 
And the production version of the material also has the BOM usage as 6 only. 

So, we would like to understand the logic behind this selection.
There are few materials with the same situation.

entry.title: ZICM Condition Type Missing in PO
entry.content:
While creating the PO with reference to PR the ZICM condition type is missing in PO condition Tab while Condition record is maintained properly. 

But While creating PO manually without reference to PR the ZICM condition type fetched automatically

entry.title: Issue in standard Internal order report kob1 and kob1n,data not as per trial balance
entry.content: KOB1 and KOB1n showing amount but as per trial balance the balance for CWIP gl is zero.

entry.title: J3RFLVMOBVEDH-Stock Overview
entry.content:
We are using the standard report J3RFLVMOBVEDH – Stock Overview and passing all the required input parameters as per the report selection screen.

However, in the report output, the values for the expected columns are not being populated. The report displays the columns, but the corresponding data is blank.

entry.title: encounter the following error message: There are no steps available.
entry.content:
Hello SAP,
We are currently in the process of configuring the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions. However, when attempting to create a purchase requisition using the APP ID F2229, we encounter the following error message: "There are no steps available. Neither existing steps nor a matching preview can be determined." This error appears on the screen prior to saving the requisition.

For more information, please check the attached document.

Regards, Rajesh Sharma

entry.title: Successor functions and display-only authorization for CJ01–03/CN21–23 in SAP S/4HANA 2025
entry.content:
We are currently upgrading our system from SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 to SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01.

We checked transactions CJ01, CJ02, CJ03, CN21, CN22, and CN23 using SU25 Step 2d.

CJ20N, Project Builder, was displayed as the successor transaction. However, none of the following indicators were set for the old transactions.

Does not exist

Locked

Obsolete

SU22 Status Old Transaction

Based on this result, we understand that these transactions remain available and are not currently classified as obsolete, although migration to CJ20N may be recommended.

Previously, authorization for create, change, and display operations was separated by transaction code as follows.

CJ01 and CN21: Create

CJ02 and CN22: Change

CJ03 and CN23: Display

If these transactions are consolidated into CJ20N, we understand that users must first be authorized to start CJ20N through S_TCODE. Create, change, and display operations within CJ20N would then be controlled through authorization objects.

In a previous internal test, we confirmed that a user could use CJ20N for display purposes only with the following authorization settings.

S_TCODE: CJ20N

C_PROJ_TCD: TRTYP = A

C_AFKO_ACT: PS_ACTVY = 03

Could you please clarify the following questions?

Are CJ01, CJ02, CJ03, CN21, CN22, and CN23 still supported and available in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01?
Is it correct to understand that these transactions are not currently scheduled for removal, but that migration to CJ20N is recommended?
When migrating a display-only user who previously used CJ03 or CN23 to CJ20N, is the SAP standard authorization concept to grant start authorization for CJ20N through S_TCODE and restrict operations to display through authorization objects such as C_PROJ_TCD and C_AFKO_ACT?
When a user with display-only authorization starts CJ20N, does CJ20N automatically operate in display mode?

Alternatively, can the user select create or change operations on the screen, with authorization checks preventing execution or saving?

Is it correct that there is no single native SAP Fiori application that provides the same complete functional scope as CJ20N?
In SAP S/4HANA 2025, what is SAP's recommended solution for the integrated creation, change, and display of Project Definitions, WBS Elements, Networks, Activities, and Material Components?

Should customers continue using CJ20N, or should they use a combination of native SAP Fiori applications?

We would also appreciate references to any relevant SAP Notes, KBAs, or official documentation.

entry.title: CK11N - inconsistent results
entry.content: We face different results for materials with a special procurement key.  Both materials are costed in the same plant and both have the same SPK defined. the same costing variant is used. Still in one case the cross-company costing is used and the other not.

entry.title: Production Confirmation defaulted as No Confirmation when done through QA32
entry.content: During confirmation of process order using 03 inspection type and QA32 transaction, pop up for confirmation is available, however default confirmation radio button is 'No Confirmation' instead of 'Partial Confirmation'

entry.title: Report RFDUML00 liefert unterschiedliche Ergebnisse
entry.content:
Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren

Bei Selektion mit einem Buchungskreis und bei der Selektion mit mehreren Buchungskreisen liefert der Bericht pro Buchungskreis unterschiedliche Ergebnisse.

frdl. grüßt,

Klaus Tartler

entry.title: Account 402101 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting
entry.content:
Hi Team,

The business is facing an issue with Sales Order 582560563. The user is attempting to post a Goods Receipt (MIGO) against PO 4530995000, but is receiving the following error:

"Account 402101 requires an account assignment relevant to cost accounting."

The user has highlighted that out of the total quantity of 6, a GR for 5 units was successfully posted in 2024 without any issues. However, while attempting to post the GR for the remaining 1 unit, the above error is being triggered.

As a temporary test, we maintained a cost element assignment in OKB9, and the posting completed successfully without any error. However, this is not the recommended approach, and we would like to understand the root cause of the issue.

Reviewd KBA  from our end -

entry.title: Interest calculation days differs in FINT transaction
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While doing interest calculation in FINT we are getting an issue with interest calculation days.

Example: Interest executed on 31.07.2026 where interest need to calculated from 01.07.2026 to 31.07.2026 where interest days are 31 days but in system it was calculating 32 days and we didn't do any enhancement for the interest calculation days. Could you please check and update

entry.title: Cannot display user field in Project builder CJ20N (Config already available)
entry.content: Cannot display user field in Project builder CJ20N (Config already available)

entry.title: Change in Update of Activity Consumption in the Quantity Strucutre from 0 to 2
entry.content:
We have business requirement to bring the actual activity consumption variances into Actual Costing.

For that we need to change our current configuration :

From 0 -No activity update to 2-Activity update relevant to price determination 

Update of Activity Consumption in the Quantitcy Strucutre

We need this effect for future no change to the historical values.

Thank you 

Maruthi

entry.title: Flexible Workflow Credit Memo Request – Unable to Upload Attachments Initial Save
entry.content:
We are experiencing a limitation with the Flexible Workflow for Credit Memo Requests in SAP.

Current Behavior

  1. During the creation of a Credit Memo Request, attachments cannot be uploaded before the document is saved and a document number is generated.
  2. Users can only add attachments after the initial save.
  3. Since the workflow is already triggered during document creation ( Approval state - 'To Be Reworked'), newly added attachments are not considered by the approvers unless the workflow is re-triggered.
  4. To re-trigger the workflow, users must make an additional change to the document (like changing the customer reference or date) so that a change log entry is written to the relevant standard tables and the workflow is triggered again.

Business Impact 

End users must perform multiple additional steps: Save the document. Upload the attachment. Modify the document again to trigger workflow re-evaluation. This process is time-consuming, inefficient, and leads to a poor user experience.

Expected Behavior

We would like to understand:

  • Whether it is possible to upload attachments during the initial creation of a Credit Memo Request before the first save and workflow trigger.
  • Whether adding an attachment after document creation can automatically trigger workflow restart/re-evaluation without requiring an additional document change.
  • If the current behavior is standard, are there any SAP-recommended configurations, enhancements, or best practices to address this scenario?

entry.title: Language update on Org BP's
entry.content: We are migrating BP's from ECC to S/4 HANA. in our dev system we have approximately 5000 org bp's with 25,000 addresses in total where the communication language is not maintained at the address level. Since this is a mandatory field in S/4 HANA, downstream processes such as move-in/move-out is affected for these bp's. Is there any standard solution provided by SAP to update the language field for these addresses in ECC or in S/4 HANA
